methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Phantom Space is a Growth-stage space and aerospace company founded by Jim Cantrell, Michael D'Angelo, and Michal Prywata. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Traditional triangulation methods proved inapplicable given data constraints. Peer-Median analysis was not executable: while 25 same-sector peers exist, filtering for stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making median-based conclusions unreliable. Public-Comp Anchoring could not proceed because Phantom Space's revenue is not on file; the public Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 42.9x P/S median across the 6 public tickers identified, but without the target's revenue figure, this multiple cannot be applied.

Without quantitative triangulation across methods, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range. The primary limiting factor is the absence of disclosed financial metrics for the target company. Resolution would require either: (1) confidential revenue or comparable financial disclosure from Phantom Space, enabling P/S or EV/Revenue anchoring; or (2) identification of at least three private comparables at identical stage and similar valuation tier to establish peer-median benchmarks. Until these data gaps are closed, valuation rests on qualitative positioning against the identified peer set rather than quantitative derivation.
